2605.22937.title: RAS: Reflection-Augmented Scaling with In-Context Learning for Executable Cypher Query Generation
2605.22937.paper_summary: Inference-time scaling can reduce errors in structured query generation, but methods to allocate the compute for query code generation remains underexplored. We study Text2Cypher, where language models generate Cypher queries that execute against property graph databases. Non-executable queries constitute a distinct syntactic failure separate from semantic inaccuracy: a syntax error triggers a system-generated error message from the database. These error messages are typically discarded at inference time rather than leveraged through in-context learning (ICL). We compare two inference methods: Independent Scaling (IS), which performs memoryless resampling, and Reflection-Augmented Scaling (RAS), which conditions each new attempt on prior execution feedback via ICL. Across three Neo4j datasets and five code-specialized language models, RAS reduces the Query Execution Error Rate by 41--50% at n{=}5, outperforming IS at 32--38%. Execution errors are not merely failures to discard but actionable feedback, and structuring inference-time compute around them is a more efficient path to executability than scaling independent samples.
2605.22937.paper_summary_zh: 推理時間的擴展可以減少結構化查詢生成中的錯誤，但分配查詢代碼生成計算的方法仍然未被充分探索。我們研究了 Text2Cypher，該模型使語言模型生成可對屬性圖數據庫執行的 Cypher 查詢。不可執行的查詢構成了一種獨特的語法失敗，與語義不準確性分開：語法錯誤會觸發數據庫生成的系統錯誤消息。這些錯誤消息通常在推理時被丟棄，而不是通過上下文學習（ICL）加以利用。我們比較了兩種推理方法：獨立擴展（IS），它執行無記憶的重採樣，以及反射增強擴展（RAS），它根據先前執行反饋通過 ICL 條件化每次新的嘗試。在三個 Neo4j 數據集和五個專門的代碼語言模型中，RAS 將查詢執行錯誤率降低了 41--50%（n{=}5），在 32--38% 的範圍內超越了 IS。執行錯誤不僅僅是丟棄的失敗，而是可操作的反饋，圍繞這些錯誤結構化推理時間計算比擴展獨立樣本是一條更有效的執行路徑。
2605.22937.author: Minseok Jung et.al.
2605.22937.authors: Minseok Jung, Abhas Ricky, Muhammad Rameez Chatni
